hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — Queen, 2, Ace. You bet. Your opponent raised you back.
Turn — Ace. Your opponent bet out. You called.
River — Jack. Your opponent shoved all-in without hesitation.

wrongReasons: A 3 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with A 3.
Q 3 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only two pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
6 4 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only one pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: A 6

Your opponent flopped three of a kind and raised the flop immediately to build the pot. Sets are too strong to slow-play — they pushed their advantage on every street.
